report_name: Microsoft 365 Admins Locked Out of Tenant After Attacker Removes All Global Admin Roles

threat_actors.name: Unidentified Actor
threat_actors.description: A malicious actor who compromised a Microsoft 365 Global Administrator account and then removed that role from all other privileged users, causing a tenant lockout.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attacker gained initial access by compromising a single legitimate user account with the 'Global Administrator' role in Microsoft 365, likely through phishing or password spraying.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Administration Command
attack_patterns.description: The attacker used standard Entra ID portal functions or PowerShell commands, such as 'Remove-MsolRoleMember', to modify role assignments and remove all other Global Administrators.

attack_patterns.name: Account Access Removal
attack_patterns.description: The attacker systematically removed the 'Global Administrator' role from all assigned user accounts to achieve a complete administrative lockout of the tenant, preventing legitimate access.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: By removing all other administrators, the attacker impaired the organization's primary defensive tool—its own IT staff—preventing them from responding to or remediating the incident.

identities.name: Unnamed Organization
identities.description: An organization that suffered a complete administrative lockout of its Microsoft 365 tenant after an attacker removed all Global Administrator roles.

indicators.name: Microsoft 365 Global Administrator Role Removal
indicators.pattern:
title: Microsoft 365 Global Administrator Role Removal
id: 2b9a8c1d-4e5f-4a3b-8c1d-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the removal of a member from the 'Global Administrator' role in Microsoft 365 / Entra ID.
  This is a critical security event that can be a precursor to or the primary action in a tenant lockout attack.
references:
  - https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/microsoft/microsoft-365-admins-locked-out-of-tenants-after-all-global-admins-removed/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-14
modified: 2026-04-14
tags:
  - attack.t1531
  - attack.impact
level: critical
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: auditlogs
detection:
  selection:
    OperationName: 'Remove member from role'
    TargetResources|contains:
      # Well-known object ID for Global Administrator role
      - '62e90394-69f5-4237-9190-012177145e10'
      - 'Global Administrator'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the removal of a user from the 'Global Administrator' role in Microsoft Entra ID audit logs. This is a high-severity action indicative of a potential tenant lockout attack.
